pattern: Two numbers only need a shared multiple no larger than the threshold to end up in the same component, so sweep multiples instead of comparing every pair.
hints: Any value larger than the threshold can never satisfy the lcm bound with anything else, so it is always its own component.

For a value at or below the threshold, walk through its multiples up to the threshold -- whichever other array value you first meet at a given multiple can be merged with it, because their lcm divides that multiple.

Drive a union-find over the array's indices with that multiples sweep; it finishes in roughly threshold*log(threshold) time, then just count the remaining distinct groups plus the always-isolated large values.

edge_inputs.description: two values whose lcm is exactly the threshold
two values whose lcm is just one above the threshold
every value exceeds the threshold, all isolated
connectivity only holds transitively through a middle value
a mix of small mutually-connected values and one large isolated value
threshold equal to one, only self-lcm-eligible values (none) can connect

stress.description: many distinct values spread across and beyond the threshold
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    threshold = rng.randint(1, 200000)
    n = max(1, min(20000, size if size >= 1 else 1))
    pool_hi = max(threshold * 2, 400000)
    pool = rng.sample(range(1, pool_hi + 1), min(n, pool_hi))
    return [pool, threshold]
